title: Collapse tldr-core/security/vuln.rs onto canonical compute_taint_with_tree via per-function loop

loc_delta_components.vuln_rs_deletions: ~1000 LOC (get_sources L140-L286, get_sinks L290-L780, scan_file_vulns substring two-pass body, extract_assigned_variable, extract_propagation, is_type_coerced, is_sanitized_sink, is_sanitized_sql, has_named_param, is_sanitized_command, get_line_at, plus 22 obsolete unit tests at L1227-L1567 + L1322-L1556 + L2077 — see reports/M1-test-enumeration.json)
loc_delta_components.vuln_rs_additions: ~450 LOC (new scan_file_vulns per-function compute_taint_with_tree dispatch loop with rayon par_iter, From-impls for canonical → vuln output adapters, vuln_type_from_sink + severity_for + descriptions_for helpers, is_parameterized_sql + is_safe_subprocess_call + has_named_param sanitization post-filters preserved as test_e2e_*-regression-guard fallbacks for argument-shape sanitizer patterns not expressible in canonical AST sanitizer dispatch)
loc_delta_components.taint_rs_additions: ~80 LOC (PHP echo_statement / print_intrinsic var-extraction extension in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast addressing M2 carry-forward-2; SSA-active indirect-match fallback in Phase 5 sink loop and flow construction loop addressing free-variable-receiver case like cursor.execute(f'...{tainted}'); AST source-bank extensions for argv[, CommandLine.arguments, Request.Query[, queryParameters[, request.getQueryString, ngx.req.get_uri_args, conn.params[ for C/Cpp/Swift/CSharp/Kotlin/Scala/Lua/Elixir; Swift system bare call_name; removed `break` in detect_sinks_ast pattern loop so PHP file_get_contents fires under both FileOpen and HttpRequest sink banks per the M2 dual-classification convention)
loc_delta_components.extract_rs_changes: +2 LOC (visibility extension fn → pub(crate) on extract_functions_detailed and extract_classes_detailed; required by validator mandate m3_uses_real_helper)
loc_delta_components.cfg_extractor_changes: +1 LOC (visibility extension fn → pub(crate) on extract_cfg_from_tree)
loc_delta_components.dfg_extractor_changes: +25 LOC (new pub(crate) extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg helper that takes a pre-built CFG to skip the inner get_cfg_context re-parse; visibility extension on extract_dfg_from_tree)

stop_thresholds_status.all_m1_red_tests_transition_green: PARTIAL — 131/166 (78.9%); ALL 83 string-literal regression-guard tests GREEN (FP-class closure mandate met); 35 positive-detection tests still RED, documented in reports/M3-carry-forward.json M3-CF-01 (Python at M4; other-language source-bank audit at a future milestone). The plan's PRIMARY closes-#24 mandate (string-literal regression-guard) is fully satisfied.
stop_thresholds_status.fall_through_languages_fp_class_regressi: PASS — 14 of 14 fall-through languages return ZERO findings on string_literal_fp fixtures
stop_thresholds_status.test_e2e_preservation_mandate: PASS — all 36 test_e2e_* tests at vuln.rs:1568-2100 GREEN (primary regression guard per validator mandate e2e_test_preservation_mandatory). val001b SSA-versioned-taint regression also GREEN (sanitizer-reassignment correctness preserved under M3's SSA-active-path indirect-match fallback's sink_var_is_ssa_tracked gate).
stop_thresholds_status.perf_two_axis_gate: FAIL — avg 17.18× M1 baseline, p99-file 5.24× M1 baseline. Documented as M3-CF-02 carry-forward per dispatch-contract M3 line 242 escape valve. Both per-file and per-function rayon parallelization applied; remaining cost is structural (per-function CFG/DFG construction + redundant whole-file AST walks per compute_taint_with_tree call).

validator_mandates_status.m3_uses_real_helper: MET — extract_functions_detailed visibility extended to pub(crate); fictional build_codemap helper not used; extract_classes_detailed also extended for languages whose method definitions live inside class/object/trait bodies (Scala, Java)
validator_mandates_status.adapter_pattern_retains_vuln_structs: MET — vuln::TaintSource and vuln::TaintSink RETAINED as output adapters with new From<canonical::TaintSource> / From<canonical::TaintSink> impls; CLI consumer at remaining/vuln.rs:679-688 reads f.source/f.sink fields unchanged
validator_mandates_status.cli_output_shape_unchanged: MET — VulnFinding / VulnSummary / VulnReport struct shapes preserved exactly; scan_vulnerabilities public API signature unchanged; JSON/SARIF schema preserved
validator_mandates_status.obsolete_test_count_authoritative_in_m1: PARTIAL — 22 obsolete vuln.rs tests deleted in M3 (required for cargo check to pass after helper deletion); CLI-side obsolete tests (test_is_taint_source, test_is_taint_sink, test_taint_tracker, test_string_interpolation_detection) deferred to M4/M5 atomic per their original scope
validator_mandates_status.e2e_test_preservation_mandatory: MET — all test_e2e_* in vuln.rs:1568-2100 retained and GREEN
validator_mandates_status.m3_perf_two_axis_gate: FAIL — but plan stop-threshold escape valve invoked: parallelization applied; remaining cost is structural and documented in M3-CF-02 carry-forward (not a regression in algorithmic correctness)

carry_forwards: M3-CF-01: 35 positive-detection RED tests remain — primarily Python (deferred to M4) and language-specific source-bank coverage gaps (deferred to a future post-M5 source-bank audit milestone). String-literal regression-guard (closes-#24) is FULLY MET.

M3-CF-02: Perf two-axis gate FAIL (avg 17.18×, p99-file 5.24×). Parallelization applied per dispatch-contract escape valve. Remaining cost is structural per-function compute_taint_with_tree dispatch — known consequence of the architectural reframe.

in_vuln_rs: get_sources fn at L140-L286 (per-language source Vec tables)
get_sinks fn at L290-L780 (per-language sink Vec tables)
extract_assigned_variable at L1029
extract_propagation at L1062
is_type_coerced at L1085
is_sanitized_sink at L1120
is_sanitized_sql at L1140
has_named_param at L1153 (re-added inline as has_named_param helper for M3 sanitization post-filter — preserves test_e2e_parameterized_query_no_findings)
is_sanitized_command at L1184
get_line_at at L1212
Pre-M3 scan_file_vulns substring two-pass body at L902-L1027

22 obsolete unit tests across L1227-L1567 + L1322-L1556 + L2077 referencing deleted helpers (per M1-test-enumeration.json — moved up from M5 ATOMIC into M3 because cargo check would fail otherwise; the dispatch-contract's expectation that M3 'cargo check passes' implicitly required this)
From<canonical::TaintSource> for vuln::TaintSource — projects var/source_type:String/line/expression
From<canonical::TaintSink> for vuln::TaintSink — projects function:String/sink_type:String/line/expression
vuln_type_from_sink(TaintSinkType) -> VulnType — exhaustive (no wildcard) classification helper
severity_for(VulnType) -> &'static str — preserves pre-M3 'HIGH' severity strings
descriptions_for(TaintSourceType, Language) -> &'static str — preserves per-language descriptive 'Flask request.args (GET parameters)' style strings (R6 mitigation)

is_parameterized_sql + has_named_param + is_safe_subprocess_call sanitization post-filters (preserve test_e2e_parameterized_query_no_findings + test_e2e_subprocess_list_no_findings; these argument-shape sanitizer patterns aren't expressible in the canonical AST sanitizer dispatch)

scan_file_vulns rewrite: parse-once-per-file + extract_functions_detailed + extract_classes_detailed enumeration (top-level fns + class methods) + per-function rayon par_iter over (extract_cfg_from_tree, extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg, compute_taint_with_tree) + From-adapter projection to VulnFinding + dedup-merge

scan_vulnerabilities perf: outer rayon par_iter over file list (effective when called with a directory; CLI dispatch invokes once per file so the inner per-function par_iter is the primary parallelism source under the current CLI architecture)
in_taint_rs: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast PHP echo_statement / print_intrinsic descent — addresses M2 carry-forward-2 (PHP echo XSS sink emission)

Phase 5 SSA-active-path indirect-match fallback — handles cursor.execute(f'...{tainted}') receiver-shaped sink case where ssa_sink_is_tainted's name-equality check structurally cannot match. Gated by !sink_var_is_ssa_tracked + !sanitized_vars.contains(sink.var) to preserve val001b sanitizer-reassignment correctness.

Flow-construction-loop indirect flow: when source.var reaches sink_block in tainted map AND its identifier is in sink.statement, accept the flow even when sink.var is structurally distinct from source.var (mirrors the indirect sink-tainted check above). Sanitizer-aware: skips when source.var or sink_var is in sanitized_vars.

AST source-bank additions for argv[ (C/Cpp), CommandLine.arguments (Swift), Request.Query[ (CSharp), queryParameters[ + call.parameters[ + call.receive< (Kotlin), request.getQueryString + queryString + body (Scala), ngx.req.get_uri_args + get_post_args + get_headers (Lua), conn.params[ + body_params[ + query_params[ (Elixir)
Swift system bare call_name added to SWIFT_AST_SINKS ShellExec

Removed break in detect_sinks_ast pattern loop — allows a single descendant to fire multiple AstSinkPattern entries (e.g., PHP file_get_contents under BOTH FileOpen and HttpRequest per M2 dual-classification convention). The downstream dedup_by(discriminant(sink_type)) filters same-type duplicates.
in_extract_rs: extract_functions_detailed visibility fn → pub(crate) (per validator mandate m3_uses_real_helper)

extract_classes_detailed visibility fn → pub(crate) (so scan_file_vulns can enumerate class-method ranges for languages where method definitions live only inside class/object/trait bodies — Scala object M { def f ... }, Java class C { void f() {} })
in_cfg_extractor_rs: extract_cfg_from_tree visibility fn → pub(crate) (avoids per-function re-parse)
in_dfg_extractor_rs: extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg new pub(crate) helper — takes a pre-built CFG so DFG construction skips an inner get_cfg_context re-parse (was the per-function quadratic cost source)

carry_forward_addressed_from_m2.carry_forward_2_php_echo_var_extraction: ADDRESSED via Option A (extend extract_first_identifier_arg_ast to walk echo_statement / print_intrinsic descendants). PHP echo XSS positive tests exercise this path; the test_e2e_php_command_injection guard validates the extension shape but doesn't directly cover echo (covered by xss_positive fixture).

wave_3_status: M3 LANDED with documented carry-forwards (M3-CF-01, M3-CF-02). All atomic-commit, output-shape, e2e-preservation, and FP-class-closure stop-thresholds MET. Perf two-axis gate FAIL but documented as known structural carry-forward per dispatch-contract M3 line 242 escape valve.
next_milestone: M4 (Python path collapse) — depends on M3
